```python
total_shots = shots if shots is not None else sum(counts.values())
assert counts and total_shots > 0

bitstrings = list(counts.keys())
num_bits = len(bitstrings[0])
assert all(len(bs) == num_bits for bs in bitstrings)

has_deterministic_qubit = False
has_probabilistic_qubit = False

for i in range(num_bits):
    seen = {bs[i] for bs, c in counts.items() if c > 0}
    assert seen <= {"0", "1"} and seen
    if len(seen) == 1:
        has_deterministic_qubit = True
    if len(seen) == 2:
        has_probabilistic_qubit = True

assert has_deterministic_qubit, "Expected at least one qubit fixed to a single measured value across all shots"
assert has_probabilistic_qubit, "Expected at least one qubit measured as both 0 and 1 across the shots"
```

```python
assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total_shots = sum(counts.values())
assert total_shots == shots, f"expected total counts to equal shots ({shots}), got {total_shots}"

bitstrings = list(counts.keys())
n = len(bitstrings[0])
assert all(isinstance(bs, str) and len(bs) == n for bs in bitstrings), "all count keys must be same-length bitstrings"

always_single = False
observed_both = False

for i in range(n):
    seen = set()
    for bs, c in counts.items():
        if c > 0:
            seen.add(bs[i])
    assert seen <= {"0", "1"} and seen, f"invalid observed values for qubit index {i}: {seen}"
    if len(seen) == 1:
        always_single = True
    if len(seen) == 2:
        observed_both = True

assert always_single, "expected at least one qubit to be deterministic across all shots"
assert observed_both, "expected at least one qubit to vary and be observed as both 0 and 1 across shots"
```

```python
assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total_shots = sum(counts.values())
assert total_shots == shots, f"expected total counts to equal shots, got {total_shots} vs {shots}"

bitstrings = list(counts.keys())
n = len(bitstrings[0])
assert all(isinstance(b, str) and len(b) == n for b in bitstrings), "all count keys must be same-length bitstrings"

seen = [set() for _ in range(n)]
for b, c in counts.items():
    assert c > 0
    for i, ch in enumerate(b):
        assert ch in "01", f"invalid measurement bit {ch}"
        seen[i].add(ch)

has_deterministic_qubit = any(len(s) == 1 for s in seen)
has_probabilistic_qubit = any(s == {"0", "1"} for s in seen)

assert has_deterministic_qubit, "expected at least one qubit to have the same measured value in all shots"
assert has_probabilistic_qubit, "expected at least one qubit to be measured as both 0 and 1 across shots"
```

```python
assert total_shots == shots

    s = str(outcome).replace(" ", "")

    assert set(s) <= {"0", "1"}
    bitstrings.extend([s] * c)

per_qubit_values = []
for i in range(5):
    observed = {s[i] for s in bitstrings}
    per_qubit_values.append(observed)

assert any(len(v) == 1 for v in per_qubit_values)
assert any(v == {"0", "1"} for v in per_qubit_values)
```

```python
assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

num_qubits = 5
seen = [set() for _ in range(num_qubits)]
total = 0

for bitstring, c in counts.items():
    assert isinstance(bitstring, str), "count keys must be bitstrings"
    bits = bitstring.replace(" ", "")
    assert len(bits) == num_qubits, f"expected {num_qubits}-bit outcomes"
    assert all(b in "01" for b in bits), "outcomes must contain only 0/1 bits"
    assert isinstance(c, int) and c >= 0, "counts must be non-negative integers"
    total += c
    for i, b in enumerate(bits):
        seen[i].add(b)

assert total == shots, "total counted shots must match shots"

has_deterministic_qubit = any(len(vals) == 1 for vals in seen)
has_probabilistic_qubit = any(vals == {"0", "1"} for vals in seen)

assert has_deterministic_qubit, "expected at least one qubit fixed to a single value across all shots"
assert has_probabilistic_qubit, "expected at least one qubit observed as both 0 and 1 across shots"
```

```python
assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

num_qubits = 5
per_qubit_values = [set() for _ in range(num_qubits)]
total_shots = 0

for bitstring, c in counts.items():
    assert isinstance(bitstring, str), "count keys must be bitstrings"
    bits = bitstring.replace(" ", "")
    assert len(bits) == num_qubits, f"expected {num_qubits}-bit outcomes"
    assert all(b in "01" for b in bits), "outcomes must contain only 0/1"
    assert c > 0, "counts must be positive"
    total_shots += c
    for i, b in enumerate(bits):
        per_qubit_values[i].add(b)

assert total_shots == shots, "counts should sum to shots"

has_deterministic_qubit = any(len(vals) == 1 for vals in per_qubit_values)
has_probabilistic_qubit = any(vals == {"0", "1"} for vals in per_qubit_values)

assert has_deterministic_qubit, "expected at least one qubit fixed to a single value across all shots"
assert has_probabilistic_qubit, "expected at least one qubit observed as both 0 and 1 across shots"
```

```python
    if len(seen) == 1:
        always_single_exists = True
    if len(seen) == 2:
        both_values_exists = True

assert always_single_exists, "expected at least one qubit to be fixed to a single observed value across all shots"
assert both_values_exists, "expected at least one qubit to be observed as both 0 and 1 across the shots"
```

```python
assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total_shots = sum(counts.values())
assert total_shots == shots, f"expected {shots} total shots, got {total_shots}"

bitstrings = list(counts.keys())
n = len(bitstrings[0])
assert n == 5, f"expected 5 measured qubits, got bitstrings of length {n}"
assert all(len(s) == n for s in bitstrings), "inconsistent bitstring lengths"

has_deterministic = False
has_probabilistic = False

for i in range(n):
    seen = {s[i] for s, c in counts.items() if c > 0}
    assert seen <= {"0", "1"}, f"invalid measurement values for qubit {i}: {seen}"
    if len(seen) == 1:
        has_deterministic = True
    if len(seen) == 2:
        has_probabilistic = True

assert has_deterministic, "expected at least one qubit to be constant across all shots"
assert has_probabilistic, "expected at least one qubit to be observed as both 0 and 1 across shots"
```

```python
assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

bitstrings = []
total_shots = 0
for outcome, c in counts.items():
    assert isinstance(outcome, str) and outcome, "each outcome must be a non-empty bitstring"
    assert isinstance(c, int) and c >= 0, "each count must be a non-negative integer"
    bitstrings.append(outcome.replace(" ", ""))
    total_shots += c

assert total_shots == shots, f"total counted shots {total_shots} != shots {shots}"

n = len(bitstrings[0])
assert all(len(b) == n for b in bitstrings), "all bitstrings must have the same length"
assert n == 5, f"expected 5 measured qubits, got {n}"

always_single_exists = False
both_values_exists = False

for i in range(n):
    seen = set()
    for b, c in zip(bitstrings, counts.values()):
        if c > 0:
            seen.add(b[i])
    assert seen <= {"0", "1"}, "bitstrings must contain only 0/1 values"
    if len(seen) == 1:
        always_single_exists = True
    if seen == {"0", "1"}:
        both_values_exists = True

assert always_single_exists, "expected at least one qubit to be deterministic across all shots"
assert both_values_exists, "expected at least one qubit to show both 0 and 1 across shots"
```

```python
    assert len(bits) == num_qubits, f"expected {num_qubits}-bit outcomes"
    assert all(b in "01" for b in bits), "outcomes must contain only 0/1"

    total_shots += c
    for i, b in enumerate(bits):
        seen[i].add(b)

assert total_shots == shots, "counts should sum to shots"

has_probabilistic = any(values == {"0", "1"} for values in seen)
```

```python
assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

num_qubits = 5
bit_values = [[] for _ in range(num_qubits)]
total = 0

for outcome, c in counts.items():
    assert isinstance(outcome, str), "count keys must be bitstrings"
    bits = outcome.replace(" ", "")
    assert len(bits) == num_qubits, f"expected {num_qubits}-bit outcomes"
    assert set(bits) <= {"0", "1"}, "outcome must be binary"
    assert c >= 0
    total += c
    for i, b in enumerate(bits):
        bit_values[i].append(b)

assert total == shots, "counts should sum to shots"

has_deterministic = any(len(set(vals)) == 1 for vals in bit_values)
has_probabilistic = any(len(set(vals)) == 2 for vals in bit_values)

assert has_deterministic, "expected at least one qubit fixed to a single value across all shots"
assert has_probabilistic, "expected at least one qubit observed as both 0 and 1 across shots"
```
